seoTitle: Carnamah vs Carnarvon — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Carnamah and Carnarvon. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Carnamah and Carnarvon.
heading: Carnamah vs Carnarvon — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Carnamah, Western Australia and Carnarvon, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Carnarvon has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Carnamah (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Carnamah or Carnarvon safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Carnamah has a flood risk score of 38/100, while Carnarvon scores 52/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Carnamah scores 18/100 for bushfire risk. Carnarvon scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Carnamah or Carnarvon?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Carnarvon has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.47% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
